title: hieracium stuppatum
side_effects: Hieracium stuppatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. Prolonged use or excessive consumption can also lead to liver damage and increased risk of bleeding. Additionally, improper handling and preparation of the plant can result in skin irritation and allergic reactions. In severe cases, Hieracium stuppatum can cause cardiovascular problems, including arrhythmias and hypotension.
